mnemonics.title: Cayley-Hamilton Remember
mnemonics.content: CHiP: Cayley-Hamilton says matrix satisfies its own Characteristic Polynomial p(A) = 0

mnemonics.title: Minimal Polynomial
mnemonics.content: MiN: Minimal polynomial is the Minimal degree Normal (monic) polynomial that Nullifies the matrix

mnemonics.title: Similar Matrices Share
mnemonics.content: SERC: Similar matrices have Equal characteristic polynomials, same Rank, and same eigenvalues (C)ounting multiplicities

acronyms.usage: States that every square matrix satisfies its own characteristic equation

acronyms.expansion: Schur Complement - Used to compute determinant of block matrices efficiently
acronyms.usage: For [A B; C D] with invertible A: det = det(A) × det(D - CA⁻¹B)

acronyms.expansion: Sum of diagonal elements equals sum of eigenvalues
acronyms.usage: tr(A) = Σλᵢ, used to find eigenvalues or verify results
det(A) = Πλᵢ, used to check invertibility (det ≠ 0)

visualTips.visualization: Imagine sliding λ down the diagonal of A, subtracting it from each diagonal element, then taking the product - like finding 'distance' from being diagonal with λ on diagonal
Think of eigenvalues as magic numbers that, when you multiply the matrix by corresponding eigenvectors, only stretch or shrink them without rotating
Draw a 2×2 grid where each cell contains a smaller matrix - eigenvalues come from diagonal blocks, off-diagonal blocks don't affect the spectrum in triangular cases
It's like finding the shortest instruction that 'cancels out' the matrix - remove repeated factors from characteristic polynomial until you find what actually kills the matrix

analogies.analogy: Like a DNA test for matrices - reveals the fundamental 'genetic' code of eigenvalues and structural properties
The shortest recipe that 'cooks' (annihilates) the matrix - removes all unnecessary ingredients from the characteristic polynomial
Like finding people in a building - eigenvalues of each floor (block) combine to give all people in the building
Same person photographed from different angles - the 'face' (properties) stays the same though the picture (matrix) looks different
